name: Methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase
summary: MTHFR encodes methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase, which converts 5,10-methylenetetrahydrofolate to 5-methyltetrahydrofolate, the methyl donor for homocysteine remethylation. The C677T variant (rs1801133) reduces enzyme activity and raises homocysteine, especially when folate intake is low. Associated with cardiovascular disease, neural tube defects, and (controversially) depression. Folate and B-vitamin status are key environmental modifiers; gene–nutrient interactions are well documented.

mechanistic_hypotheses.hypothesis: TT genotype increases homocysteine; low folate intake amplifies the effect and cardiovascular risk.

references.title: MTHFR 677C>T and cardiovascular disease
references.journal: Journal of the American Medical Association
